review_title: Fantastic email marketing software with great workflows
description: Overall we've had a very positive experience with Iterable that has given me autonomy over email marketing and have had not to rely too much on developers to help me achieve my email marketing goals.
author: Alan C.
pros: The WYSIWYG editor for emails is really nice because I can build emails without any code and make them look on brand and functional. The workflow tool is also really powerful for building automatic campaigns that continually perform well.
cons: Iterable feels like it just slow sometimes. Loading campaigns, saving templates, navigating to different parts of the software. If the pages and elements loaded faster it would feel a lot more snappy.

review_title: My Experience with Iterable Explained in a Review
author: Andrew M.
pros: Iterable enhances cross-channel marketing campaigns orchestration which unify customer experiences in real-time. The availability of robust personalization and segmentation tools is an added advantage since it is easy to segment audiences based on location and behaviors.
cons: There has really been nothing that I encounter as a flaw when using Iterable for graphic designs campaigns.

description: In overall, Iterable is a nice tool to have in company that allow us to understand better our customer base.
author: Flavio M.
pros: Nice tool that has an interface really ease to use. Iterable records various customer events, which are visible in two formats - charts and table listing, which helps us to understand better customer's behaviour and target marketing campaigns, also manage targeted emails sent to customers.
cons: Sometimes if the number of messages sent to Iterable are high, their API gets overwhelmed and we can get some error messages returned.

description: While the onboarding (outsourced) experience and sales experience was good, I find myself struggling with basic things as setting up new senders. We have been blocked twice from sending SMS, and they don't seem to move very quickly on such business critical issues either. I am weary of signing on new products with them, because I also don't know which parts of the tool will be free to use, and what I would have to pay for after I have signed. You should really probe into that aspect if you're looking to onboard them.
author: Naina T.
pros: Super flexible, allows lots of customization. Good for scale. Onboarding process was quick and helpful.
cons: - Aftersales service. We signed up for their email and SMS 5 months ago. Our SMS have been blocked twice in the past 1 month, and they have zero accountability for it. They refuse to tell us why they block us, or what we can do to prevent it in the future. - Hidden fee - they charge extra for something as simple as creating new senders, something which is usually self serve in even the most basic tools and they don't tell you this when signing you on. - Reporting - not reliable. You get different numbers when you open different reports. It took us multiple emails to tell them there was an issue when our unique open rates were higher than open rates.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great tool for managing marketing channels at scale
description: Great tool hoping it continues to outperform competitors in the space who are ever evolving and improving their product but for now would recommend it as one of the better marketing tools for email and texting.
author: Tim D.
pros: Iterable is a versatile tool that I've used for 9+ years for marketing campaigns. It's workflow capabilities are by far the most seamless setup compared to the competition. Email and texting all with one tool making campaign setup easy and straight forward.
cons: Occasional support issues and minor bugs that sometime take a bit to get the answers on what happened (nearly every tool will have these problems).

description: Complete tool which supports communication with customers, cheap with high ROI
author: Kara S.
pros: Connecting with our customer across email, sms, web and mobile with in-app messaging Email marketing through creating and sending target email campaigns Data collection from customers in real time and management all in one place
cons: It was complex for me to learn and start using it since i had little knowledge in marketing automation

review_title: Great marketing automation solution for seamless customer engagement.
author: David O.
pros: I like how it allows businesses to engage customers across various touchpoints.
cons: Some of it’s advanced features may have steep learning curve.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: There is no one tool in marketing automation that is perfect, but Iterable checks the most boxes today out of any of them. Customer support is fantastic. Segmentation is fantastic. UI is improving. The tool's overall power for users with little technical background is superb.
cons: I think the reporting tools could be a bit better. Supports MMS through Twilio would also be a huge bonus.

description: The ability to deliver personalization data to customers and easy communication management Iterable provides the most reliable functions.
author: Silvia K.
pros: Very powerful intelligent personalization system and very helpful dynamic content creation platform which offers excellent integration capability. List management through the platform is incredible and the mobile optimization Iterable is the great choice.
cons: None at all for Iterable and its tools, since the first training is very easy and to customize the features is also easy process.

review_title: Great platform while being a value for money.
description: Overall it is a good, easy to use and user friendly software which does not require and pre requisite knowledge in order to use it. Faster than other platforms and value for money as well.
author: Mohit S.
pros: It is a value for money and easy to use software. Gives you a great learning curve while utilizing it on a day to day basis.
cons: Its customer support can be improved as response time is way more. Response time of the support is annoying as they they more time to resolve any issues.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Victoria S.
pros: I like how it's a cross channel integrated platform, meaning it's now our one-stop-shop for all comms.
cons: There's a lot of features in the platform and it took us a while to understand and utilise properly.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Multi-Channel Marketing has eased our marketing experience while it ensures that we reach all our customers and potential ones at the same time. Also,
author: Ciro R.
pros: Marketing automation is a great feature in Iterable which ensures that you never lose any marketing chance you have
cons: It is quite pricey So many clicks to get where you want

description: Overall, I really love to use Iterable because this software is really user-friendly.
author: Jr L.
pros: I really like the support team of this software. To be honest, this software is not that hard to learn but there are times that you feel like your at the end of the road and cannot get the outcome you need to have for your report but there's their support who will share some tips and tricks and sometimes links from their support page/help docs.
cons: I only dislike the part of the software where the user fields sometimes looks confusing as there are lots of details that can be found in it.

description: Super positive, I love the features Iterable provides. All in one package for Marketers
author: Volodymyr B.
pros: Ease of use, automations features, integrations with other platforms.
cons: I think, drag and drop editor can be a bit improved. Maybe ability to add custom build blocks to drag and drop editor (kinda like HubSpot dynamic templates)

description: Automatic blame deflection of any issues we've raised, canned responses and total failure to meet promised value
author: Kim B.
pros: Cart abandoner targeting promised, non-engineer self-service of complex marketing and retargeting campaigns.
cons: Blaming Segment for failure to merge anonymous events with identified users, blaming Segment for integration failures that caused significant loss of data. Awful limitations of workflows. No ability of non-engineers to actually successfully self-service on pretty much any features. Zero interest on actually enabling us to leverage the service to create value

review_title: A lot of cutting edge features, maybe too advanced
author: Sheel C.
pros: The behavioral based segmentation features are very powerful and put a lot of power from the engineering/analytics teams into the marketing team. Behavioral based segmentation, dynamic lists, and large workflows all have a lot flexibility but that can cause errors in incorrect lists/segments/emails.
cons: An ESP has to encompass so many features to be useful and there are some that Iterable doesn't have or have just recently launched, such as an analytics dashboard to see how campaigns are performing against each other. Iterable has definitely focused on the advanced segmentation and workflows, but still has to build a lot of other core features that email marketers will find necessary.

review_title: Excellent for workflows, but lacking in other areas
description: I really like Iterable and I think they're on to a winner, it does feel like they're still in early stages as not everything feels completely ironed out
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The workflow functionality is really great and offers a lot of opportunities for CRM management
cons: Certain features don't function as you'd expect them to, and report stats aren't as clear and easy to use as competitors.

review_title: It makes email marketing so easy!
author: George W.
pros: It's easy to use and understand. It's also very easy for me to create an email template for the rest of the team to use. The feature of multiple accounts is also a great way to easily switch between teams.
cons: There's a few bugs here and there, but the support team are always happy to help me out! I do wish there was an easy way for me to upload images for the email templates.

review_title: Its a good software for sending different types of emails. We use it for sending marketing campaigns
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to use setup and configurations. We can easily categorize and send emails based on the categories. Like marketing, transactions.
cons: I don't think any. I like everything about this product and I recommend to others also to use this product.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Overall a very good experience using Iterable
description: - email marketing - web push - workflow creations
author: Himanshu S.
pros: New to iterable. Using iterable for Email marketing and workflow creation. Overall very good experience with iterable. Nice iterface, easy to use, effective in email marketing - Easy to use as compare to other softwares like Salesforce - Great Product for email marketing - Easy to integrate with APIs
cons: New to iterable and it looks complex initially, but later its very easy once you start using it. It would have been better if anaytics can be better, better visualizations.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Sonia B.
pros: Flexibility with any type of desired build out, always improving their product
cons: Problems with adding new users to lists and with clients that are less digital-savy to navigate the backend on their own

author: Nanita Rica Winniewski W.
pros: I like it because it has academy for training and certifying users.
cons: Iterable is expensive to purchase and it has to improve on price

description: Very good, but it was a bit expensive for our budget.
author: Fred F.
pros: Easy to use. Very reputable platform and great troubleshooting
cons: It was a touch too expensive for our budget.

author: Jade Anthony R.
pros: You can book or request a demo in their website, and uses AI for convenience.
cons: Pricing is not display on their website.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: When I first started using Iterable at my new job, I wasn't a fan. The capabilities were restricted especially in terms of analytics compared to other CRM tools. However, over the last 3 years, they have been adding new features slowly and catching up to other tools. My main problem with Iterable is regarding its UX and usability. As an everyday user, I find Iterable hard to use as you need too many clicks to achieve anything you are doing. I work fast and need to be able to generate campaigns swiftly. Iterable doesn't allow this and you sometimes need to click 5-6 different buttons to open a window.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is a simple tool that's easy to use and easy to figure out. Great for simple use cases. Email template building experience and features are better than other CRM tools.
cons: The platform is very click-dependant, meaning that you need so many clicks to perform a single function. Navigation is complex. Doesn't allow much flexibility when setting up campaigns.
